author: @SSGrille
text:
What happens if you take out the springs under the horseshoe arm, and replace them with a cam that would raise and lower the magnet to coincide with the correct timing. Or can you counter weight the drum, or shape it like a cam, where as it speeds up as the magnetic force pulls harder, the inertia can kick it over the top like old steam locomotive wheels?

Makes me think about the design Tesla says he came up with to start something resonating, then adding just a little more energy to that pulse each time it came back. The story of his “earthquake machine” I think was the name of it, school was sooooo long ago.

Then inside the drum with the magnets, install your copper coil so instead of an additional device being driven, it is all part of the same unit. I am really curious now how the angled strip of magnets interact with a copper coil that is all the same “width” across the drum. With a changing magnetic field, I expect less as the magnets are further apart, and more as they are closer together, do you get a variation in the voltage out, in essence shoving the voltage through the coil in a building pulse? Maybe all our magnetic fields have been wrong, and by compressing them “down the coil” we can get just that extra push, like you pressing your finger on the horseshoe, to add just that tiny bit of energy to the system to keep it spinning. 

Always appreciate how you get my mind thinking about doing something different.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: these are good ideas mate - give them a go - have a go at building it and finding your own answers

author: @getsmartr
text:
This is very interesting.  If the  "finger" magnet were an electromagnet,  it wouldn't need to move.  It could be timed by a hall effect sensor and powered by the generator output.  A spring loaded timing advance from an old car could handle high speed activation.  

Also using the hall effect sensor means that the timing will always be on,  even if the rotor speed is variable throughout the cycle. 

Now the next question is,  what to use for input?

author: @jesterlampoon-t1j
text:
How could you maximize the amount of energy you get from 1 rotation? It looked like you were able to get not quite 10v?
I've pondered this idea for a while: Have the spring magnet be permanently fixed in the down position, then have an electromagnet on top of it - turning it into a pseudo "permanent magnet switch." Once it reaches the field's hump, have a capacitor discharge the built up current into the electromagnet (turning "off" the permanent magnet) to get it over the hump. 
I'm sure it wouldn't be perpetual, but I would think it would last for quite a while depending on the capacitor.

author: @FuhrChris
text:
I've been working on some things here trying to get ready for larger Sciency projects only inside a new building.
Last week we actually had enough rain that the grid of channels in the foundation dirt has copied the Egyptian method marking out for level where the laser level was off.  (The ground was dug flattened, it just was not perfected as level it is off by about 2 inches over 60 feet.)  (When the rains here stop here for the season I will go back out.  Dig a hole deep enough to place the laser level and then get the foundation even more refined.)
(When the Monsoon season is over here the ground dry's out to the point where it's near onto rock hard, and you literally scrape the ground flat.) 

I'm having problems trying to design in several different systems that would work together in the area of heating and cooling let alone trying to figure out which system or how much Hybrid collection of several systems will be the best based primarily on costs.  (Transportations to project site is a Major issue out here.)

author: @FuhrChris
text:
So, ...
How would you make 3 or more liner moving rods work in successions and retain timing to each other?

You could base each pushing rod on a prefect triangle arrangement with the reduction gear tucked out of the way?
Or
you could make 3 or more V gears cylinders (that's a whole lot more magnets) that all spin onto the reduction gear?
Or 
You could make both in which you could have more than 9 pushing rods that move 3 V gear cylinders that each of the V Gears would contribute torque onto the reduction gear arranged in a configuration that looks something like a perverted Sun gear arrangement. (The 3 push rods would have to be off set in something closer to an Old Radial Airplane engine, that has 3 V gear boxes in a triangle configuration around your final power transfer shaft.) 

So if the 3 or 9 push rods were each connected to a Sterling engine then you would have a way to convert the small piston low energy system into a collected power arrangement.  Thus having a way around the issue of Graphite pistons and Lab glass tubes.  Since you can't get the high precision walled glass in large sizes very economically.  
The Sterling engines external collection plates (Pie shaped, or the circle that rests on coffee cup arrangement, only a larger chamber) would then be radiated from the center into 9 different collectors', like some form of a clock with only 9 positions.  (Ya, you could do 12 pistons if you wanted a 12 position clock configuration.)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: build the model mate

replies.author: @FuhrChris
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering I will wait till I'm back working with the current version of Rhino.
Zbrush is great for doing things like adding low reliefs in a Victorian theme onto the surface of a build.
But
Making things that require dimensioning and specific dimensions' for issues like gears meshing properly it's hit or miss.  (Mostly miss!)

I'm still off grid.
The batteries in the Solar panel system have completely failed. 
I'm on a Honda Generator 2200 for all electricity right now.
You know first hand how important having a dependable supply of electricity for long enough for a 3D print is important.
The Honda would require that I refill the gas tank every 4 hours while it's running for any decent sized prints.  (This includes setting (2) alarm clocks and getting up twice in the middle of the night to do re-fuelings.   

Once the shed has been buttoned up for the Winter, I can focus on building a Battery house for the enlarged (changing from 12v to 24v) system and I will be trying for the target of "3 days of grey" level of off grid power.
It will be after that I can focus on improving additive manufacturing, and CAD/CAM.

author: @scotttovey
text:
In addition to being a generator, that has the makings of being a drive motor, but you would need a mechanical way to control the rhythm.

This can be done easily by lengthening the two rods holding the horse shoe.

Then add a cross beam with a solenoid on it, and have it triggered the horse shoe using a reed switch and magnet.

At this point the question would be; how much energy is used to depress the horseshoe versus how much energy is produced by the generator.

But be careful, you don't want to accidentally build a machine that produces more energy than it is using.

The most efficient design I can think of is to insure the weight of the solenoid, equals the weight of the force of the springs and the magnets. Or slightly under it.

I'm thinking in terms of counter weights that are used to hold a window sash in place regardless of where you open the window too. But an overhead solenoid may not work in that way.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: counterweights an certainly an interesting idea - cheers mate

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
"counterweights an certainly an interesting idea - cheers mate"

Yes, but would it be the most efficient?

If the horse shoe is perfectly balanced with counterweights, it would technically be, in that system, weightless.

It would then require the solenoid to move the horse shoe out of the thrust position and back into the thrust position, requiring two pulses.

If the horse shoe and counter weights are perfectly balanced, would that then require the solenoid to hold the horse shoe in the thrust position, thereby defeating the purpose of counter weights?

Which would be most efficient?
Two pulses: one moving the horseshoe out of the thrust position and the other one moving the horseshoe in the thrust position?
Or a single pulse which overcomes the springs holding the horse shoe down in the thrust position?

I think I just disproved my original idea.
LOL

author: @danielfeyerabend904
text:
Hello, Good Doctor.  
I'll try to find those prints and add some mods to them.
Here are some improvements: 
Reverse the spring force to push the drive magnet into instead of away from the Vs.  Add a lever to push the drive magnet out of the gates.  Use a small solenoid to actuate the lever.  This can be timed with a hall sensor and positioned magnets.  
This might make a very curious device.

author: @russellzauner
text:
omg
I think you just solved a potential issue with my tricycle mag brake design I was considering the past couple days or so.
if the two "plates" containing magnets (calipers) generating a field arranged as such are rotated from each other then the force changes upon the "rotor" (your wheel) very disproportionately to the force needed to rotate the shaped field plates to generate the force.   So when I squeeze my "brakes" handle, one of the plates simply rotates in a very measurable calibratable and controllable manner with a lot less effort from my grip than if I had to squish two plates together horizontally and be directly opposing the end force generated itself.  That actually solves a whole slew of what was complex mechanical math and simplifies operation as well as reduce parts count quite a bit.

Thanks!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: That sounds like a great idea mate, glad the video helped!

replies.author: @JD2jr.
replies.text: wouldn't a regular water wheel be way more efficient and less complicated?

replies.author: @Bill-n9l7d
replies.text: ​​@JD2jr.Yes but that would not be the point nor would that make an interesting conversation piece for the yard. An invention is not always about being efficient. As far as that goes if I wanted a more effencent way to use water for power I would not use a water wheel.

replies.author: @Bill-n9l7d
replies.text: ​@JD2jr.I was going to add to my comment to you by edit but decided to just make another comment. The point is like what happens in that lunar mission where the filtering system for the oxygen generator malfunctioned and needed replacement in outer space. There was no replacement ....the damage was unexpected. So NASA engineers saved everyone's life on board by taking things that were available in the capsule. Instructing the astronauts. They even use a sleeve from one of the space suits to make a duct tube. It fulfilled a function in a very elaborate way. When the most effective parts could not be found. They made one anyway. And that's the point. Don't throw the baby out with the wash.

author: @TimeSurfer206
text:
Having power available does 2 things: It either amplifies my work, or it automates it, leaving me free to do other things.
If these don't explain our obsession with power, nothing will.
I can think of a couple uses for this little varmint.  Exemplum gibt: If it could be scaled down dramatically, I can see it being a nice converter for a Thermoacoustic Engine. It'd also be useful for a wave action generator, and that's just the beginning, right?

author: @weAre0ButManyInHere

Could each north south oriented magnet pair on the drum be alternated sequentially, according to the their rotating order, for stator coils & still induce the drum to spin? Maby speculation, that you could add more "horse shoe" attractor magnet bars radially equally spaced around the drum each synchronized to their optimal heights above the v magnet array using a crank & pushrods, thinking that you could increase the amount of turning power that your prime mover's linear occillations can exert on the drum, maby such speculation could enhance? Fascinating and informative channel ✌️

author: @savage1r
text: I thought you might find this interesting and I just tested and confirmed this, this morning. You can take a water distiller that can also distil alcohol. You can get low temperature windshield wiper fluid for cheap and distil it into methanol. And it's SUPER hot AND clean burning.

replies.author: @ferrumignis
replies.text: It wouldn't be worthwhile in Europe as screenwash is limited to maximum 0.6% methanol because it's quote toxic.  Most of the alcohol in European approved screenwash is Ethanol or IPA, and only about 40-50% is alcohol, the rest if water and surfactants.  A 5L container of concentrated screenwash costs about the same as 5L of Methanol, Ethanol or IPA.

replies.author: @frankcarden4709
replies.text:
Any fruit even toxic to humans can be fermented and distilled,
About1/4 of a bucket of fruit about 4lbs of sugar, and water from a well or stream so there is no bleach in it.....cos that'll kill your yeast, and about 2 teaspoons of yeast.
Scalled the fruit first to kill wild yeast and bugs,make sure everything is sterilised before you start (any unspecified bacteria can turn it to poop).
Make sure the fruit is squished and mixture is upto or a little below body temp before adding the yeast and mix well.
Cover it to keep flys out and mix it every day for a week,come back in two weeks to syphon off the top leaving the sediment in the bottom.
Oh and leave atleast 4 inches room in th bucket for expansion.
Then distill.
If you wana use it to get merry, don't use toxic berry's just to be safe and discard the first half shotglass, as this will have the most bad alcohol that you can't metabolise ( can't remember if it's ethanol or methanol).
Or don't distill and you've got a crude wine!

replies.text: Would that not just be friction? The whole point of the magnets (and the bar by extension!) is to make it efficent by not actually having a contact surface between the 2 components. Otherwise you could use good ol' crankshafts like how they were used even on ancient steam engines.

author: @owenminor
text: You could take this to another level, by interspersing. As you describe a mechanism, you describe a sub mechanism that has already been used somewhere in history. Taking a small pause, out in the scenery of the world, to talk about how people used a sub-design of the master part and how it changed history would be a really cool sub-cut in your telling of the story of the new "what is that?" final design. Ending the episode on "Who used it and where the heck was this used?" is a good cliffhanger for an episode.
